title: Missile production push runs into solid rocket motor bottleneck
summary: A CSIS report highlights that the planned 2027 interceptor purchases will strain a supply chain for solid rocket motors, which is still recovering from years of consolidation. The bottleneck threatens missile production efforts.

title: Revised Artemis lunar lander plans take shape
summary: NASA has outlined updated plans for the Artemis lunar landers, with Blue Origin and SpaceX refining their approaches to accelerate development. The revisions aim to meet Artemis mission timelines more effectively.

title: SpaceX shares rise nearly 20% in historic IPO
summary: SpaceX shares surged nearly 20% on their first day of trading following the company's historic IPO, marking a significant milestone for both SpaceX and the space industry.

title: Avanti trims GEO exposure with Hylas-3 sale
summary: Avanti Communications is selling its Hylas-3 satellite to reduce its geostationary orbit (GEO) exposure. This move marks a shift away from its previous debt-fueled expansion strategy.

title: Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
summary: Astronomers warn that SpaceX's planned orbital data center satellites, set to launch as early as next year, could interfere with astronomical observations. The concern highlights potential disruptions to scientific research due to the increasing presence of commercial space infrastructure.

title: All in on AI at Astra
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp suggests he might be the sole employee due to the company's AI-focused strategy. The company previously participated in the SPAC boom.

title: H3 successfully returns to flight
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight on June 11, placing six small satellites into orbit. This marks its first mission since a failure in December.

title: In aerospace, AI isn’t replacing workers. It’s filling a shortage
summary: Aerospace executives view AI as a solution to workforce shortages rather than a replacement for human workers, highlighting the industry's stretched industrial base. The focus is on leveraging AI to address labor gaps in the aerospace sector.

title: AAC Clyde Space wins ESA contract to complete maritime-monitoring constellation
summary: AAC Clyde Space secured a €10.9 million contract from the European Space Agency to develop and demonstrate VDES satellites for maritime monitoring. The contract aims to complete a maritime-monitoring constellation.

title: Vandenberg offers new launch site for small and medium rockets
summary: The U.S. Space Force is offering a new launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base for small and medium rockets. This initiative aims to attract potential users for the facility.

title: 'We were astonished': Millions of exoplanets could be born near active supermassive black holes
summary: Millions of exoplanets could form near active supermassive black holes, according to a new study. Researchers expressed surprise at the potential scale and size range of planet formation in such extreme environments.

title: Millions could see a rare sunset during the total solar eclipse on Aug. 12, 2026. Here's where to look
summary: A total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, will be visible across parts of the Northern Hemisphere, offering a rare sunset-like experience. Observers in specific regions should plan ahead to witness this event.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 214 — Moon Man
summary: The 214th episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine about his past and the future of the space agency. The conversation covers his experiences and insights on NASA's trajectory.

title: How Japanese scientists sent a real-life Transformer to the moon
summary: Japanese scientists deployed an autonomous rolling rover that transformed from a sphere into a robot to explore the moon in 2024. This marks a significant achievement in lunar exploration technology.

title: Ever have a scary HR meeting on your calendar? That's how the Artemis 3 crew found out their assignments
summary: NASA used an unconventional method to inform the Artemis 3 crew of their assignments during a scheduled HR meeting. The astronauts learned of their roles in a surprise announcement.

title: SpaceX's stock wasn't the company's only launch today — It also put 29 Starlink satellites in orbit
summary: SpaceX launched 29 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket, coinciding with its public debut on the NASDAQ stock market.

title: Japan's H3 rocket bounces back from failure with successful return to flight launch carrying 6 satellites
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight after a previous failure, carrying six satellites. The mission marks a significant recovery for the country's space program.

title: Could the secret to black hole formation be locked away in this record-breaking ancient quasar?
summary: A record-breaking ancient quasar from 12.9 billion years ago suggests supermassive black holes aged rapidly in the early universe. This discovery could hold clues to black hole formation.

title: NASA to Cover 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission Space Station Departure
summary: NASA will cover the departure of SpaceX's Dragon spacecraft from the International Space Station on June 16, returning scientific research samples and hardware to Earth. Live coverage will be available on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA's YouTube channel.

title: Black Eye Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes captured a composite image of the Black Eye Galaxy (Messier 64) in March 2026, combining data from multiple wavelengths. The galaxy was observed in near- and mid-infrared by Webb and ultraviolet, visible, and near-infrared by Hubble.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees. This observation falls under the science category.

title: NASA Award Boosts Space Technology Research Capabilities
summary: NASA opened a new funding opportunity called M-STAR to accelerate academic research and technology development. The application window closes on August 11.

title: NASA’s Chandra Discovers Possible Supernova Remnant in Galactic Center
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory has potentially discovered a supernova remnant near the center of the Milky Way galaxy. The findings, published in The Astrophysical Journal, could provide insights into the distribution of heavy elements in the galaxy.

title: I Am Artemis: Elkin Norena
summary: Elkin Norena, NASA's resident management officer for the Space Launch System Program, has contributed to over a dozen space shuttle missions. These missions included sending crews to low Earth orbit and supporting historic spaceflights.

title: NASA Robotic Tech Demo Will Advance Prototype Gamma-Ray Detectors
summary: NASA is advancing prototype gamma-ray detectors through a robotic arm demonstration on its upcoming Fly Foundational Robots mission. This technology demo aims to enhance gamma-ray sensor capabilities for future space applications.

title: Soccer Meets Space Science
summary: A soccer ball was tested aboard the International Space Station to study how internal mass affects motion in microgravity. The research aims to improve understanding of embedded technologies in sports equipment.

title: NASA’s Career Technical Education Day Highlights Technical Careers
summary: NASA hosted Career Technical Education Day to highlight technical careers and cultivate the next generation of skilled workers. The event emphasized the critical role of technicians in advancing NASA's research and missions.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test following Flight 12's completion three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, and LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission is part of the agency's preparations for future lunar landings.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing for Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink, including three Starlink missions. Flights are scheduled from California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the loss of its New Glenn rocket during a hotfire test at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life beneath the Martian surface while adhering to planetary protection protocols.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry, which still relies heavily on private funding. The move could attract more investment and validate the sector's growth potential.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the kind of economy that will emerge from this capital influx. The largest IPO in history is poised to drive record investment, but its impact on operational versus hardware-focused outcomes remains uncertain.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a significant event for the global space industry, with implications for Europe's space sector. The listing in New York marks a major milestone for a US space company.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox has launched an AI-focused update to enhance the capabilities of AI agents in geospatial-data analysis. This update aims to improve the effectiveness of AI-driven geospatial solutions.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on the accuracy of SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO, though they agree the space industry is experiencing significant growth.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC Sat, entering the orbital data center market. The company aims to leverage space-based infrastructure for data processing and storage.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital has raised $5 million in a pre-seed funding round to develop its ODC constellation. The ODC concept uses a network of smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches. The decision aims to balance regulatory flexibility with encouraging timely deployment.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for the Artemis III mission's Earth orbit test in 2027, serving as a precursor to future Moon landings. The event marks a key collaboration between NASA and private companies for lunar exploration.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has chosen Arkadia Space, a Spanish startup, to supply a propulsion system for its 2027 satellite mission. This collaboration highlights the growing role of emerging space companies in supporting major satellite initiatives.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price, valuing the company at over $150 billion. This surge in stock price made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is emerging as critical infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge. This integration is becoming essential for managing fleets, utilities, industrial sites, and remote assets.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for improved disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to leverage Starlink's satellite internet for global emergency preparedness.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded Earth Observation (EO) satellite contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance its space capabilities.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet as part of the PTS-G Swarm 1 mission. This contract marks a significant step in Viasat's expansion in the military satellite communications sector.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, leveraging investor confidence in Elon Musk and anticipated AI growth. The company, which has operated privately for 24 years, aims to capitalize on its space and AI ventures.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has exited stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build this network to support future space operations.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge has secured £10 million ($13.4 million) in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding is part of ESA's General Support Technology Programme, facilitated through UKSA's investment.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their collaboration in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The partnership aims to enhance mutual security and technological cooperation in the space sector.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation data company, is expanding its operations into Brazil, citing the country's significant climate pressures and rapid growth as key factors. The move marks a strategic push into one of Latin America's largest markets.
